additional_rules.name: debug-mode-in-production
additional_rules.message: Never ship debug=True — leaks the Werkzeug debugger and arbitrary code execution to production.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Set debug from an environment variable: app.run(debug=os.environ.get('FLASK_DEBUG') == '1'). Production deployments should use gunicorn/uWSGI, not app.run().

additional_rules.name: hardcoded-secret-key
additional_rules.message: SECRET_KEY hardcoded — load from os.environ or a secret manager.
additional_rules.fix_hint: SECRET_KEY = os.environ['SECRET_KEY']  # raises KeyError early if missing. Generate with secrets.token_urlsafe(64).

additional_rules.name: raw-sql-string-concat
additional_rules.message: Raw SQL with string concatenation — SQL injection risk. Use bound parameters with sqlalchemy.text(...).
additional_rules.fix_hint: db.session.execute(text('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = :id'), {'id': user_id}). Never concatenate user input into the query string.

additional_rules.name: session-cookie-secure-disabled
additional_rules.message: SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=False — session cookie may be sent over HTTP and intercepted.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Set SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True in production config classes; only ProdConfig should ever set it False (e.g. for local development).

additional_rules.name: csrf-exempt-on-form-route
additional_rules.message: csrf.exempt on a POST route — verify this endpoint uses token auth, not session cookies, before exempting CSRF.
additional_rules.fix_hint: If the route uses Flask-Login session auth, do not exempt CSRF — keep the form's csrf_token. Only exempt routes that authenticate via Authorization header / token.

additional_rules.name: before-first-request-removed
additional_rules.message: before_first_request was removed in Flask 2.3 — runtime error on Flask 2.3+.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Move the startup logic into create_app() so it runs at app construction, or wrap it in `with app.app_context(): ...` and call from a CLI entry point.

additional_rules.name: flask-markup-import
additional_rules.pattern: from flask import [^\n]*\bMarkup\b|from flask import Markup
additional_rules.message: flask.Markup was deprecated in Flask 2.3 and removed in Flask 3.0.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Import Markup from markupsafe instead: `from markupsafe import Markup`. Flask re-exports it via that package.

additional_rules.name: module-level-flask-app
additional_rules.message: Module-level Flask(__name__) prevents per-test app construction and conditional extension wiring.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Wrap construction in a create_app(config_name) factory. Initialize extensions with init_app(app) inside the factory.

additional_rules.name: business-logic-in-route
additional_rules.message: Business logic helper defined in routes/views — move to a service module.
additional_rules.fix_hint: Create app/{feature}/services.py and move the logic there. The route should validate input, delegate to the service, and return a response.

additional_rules.name: missing-eager-load
additional_rules.message: Loop over a query result — verify relationships are eager-loaded with selectinload()/joinedload() to prevent N+1.

additional_rules.name: print-in-app-code
additional_rules.message: print() in application code — use app.logger or logging.getLogger(__name__) instead.
additional_rules.fix_hint: logger = logging.getLogger(__name__); logger.info(...). Configure structured JSON output in production.

additional_rules.name: hardcoded-url
additional_rules.message: Hardcoded URL — use url_for('blueprint.endpoint', **params) instead so URL changes do not break callers.
additional_rules.fix_hint: redirect(url_for('users.show', user_id=u.id)) or <a href="{{ url_for('users.show', user_id=u.id) }}">

additional_boundaries.name: Routes must not import models from other features directly
